meaning: adj.疲惫不堪的,操劳过度的
examples: Prolonged strike action debilitated the industry.
Stewart took over yesterday when Russell was debilitated by a stomach virus.
So why are Europe’s leaders so debilitated?
He lived the rest of his life in obscurity, traumatized and debilitated by his war experience.
Certainly some women suffer more than others, debilitated by heavy bleeding, painful cramps and migraines.
